scope: AP15 Option C -- shared CacheBackend Protocol covering FeatureCache, DiscoveryCache, SuiteArtefactCache (extend feature_handling/cache_backend.py; migrate two facades; microbench)

deferred_aspects_with_reason.reason: SuiteArtefactCache uses safe_pickle.safe_dump (atomic .pkl + .pkl.sha256 in one operation). DiscoveryCache uses manual pickle.dump + os.fsync + os.replace, then separate sidecar write with sidecar-failure logged at DEBUG and treated as non-fatal. Unifying loses one or the other documented contract.

DiscoveryCache.set explicitly fsyncs the cache directory on POSIX after rename (Audit D L-10 fix); SuiteArtefactCache + FeatureCache do not. Routing all writes through one backend either grants every caller the dir-fsync cost (regression for FeatureCache hot path) or drops it from DiscoveryCache (durability regression on journaled-data-mode-off filesystems).

Three different choices in production: FeatureCache uses PIDAwareFileLock (mlframe-internal wrapper with stale-lock reclaim), DiscoveryCache uses raw filelock.FileLock with contextlib.nullcontext fallback when filelock missing (documented optional-dep behaviour), SuiteArtefactCache uses no cross-proc lock (threading.Lock only, relies on os.replace atomicity). A single backend must commit to one; each caller currently has a documented reason for its choice.

FeatureCache + DiscoveryCache: .lru JSON sidecar, file-locked, persists across process restart, cross-process consensus. SuiteArtefactCache: in-memory OrderedDict only, rebuilt from mtimes on first access -- Wave 8 deliberately chose mtime-based eviction to avoid every put/get paying for JSON read-modify-write under a filelock. A unified backend cannot serve both without an lru_persistence config switch that effectively forks the implementation into the same three code paths inside one file.

DiscoveryCache.__init__ raises ValueError when both max_entries=None and max_size_mb=None (auditable opt-in for unbounded growth). SuiteArtefactCache silently accepts bytes_limit=0 + max_entries=None as 'no caps'. FeatureCache LocalDiskBackend accepts max_entries=None + max_size_mb=None as zero-config workload. Protocol cannot enforce all three contracts; pushing checks up duplicates the LRU/eviction code path Option C was meant to centralise.

DiscoveryCache._safe_key (pure-hex pass-through, blake2b+length-tag for non-hex) is the only sanitiser. FeatureCache + SuiteArtefactCache assume callers pass hex. Hoisting into a shared backend changes the on-disk key namespace for the other two; leaving it in the DiscoveryCache facade makes the Protocol asymmetric and defeats the centralisation goal.

behavioural_equivalence_sensor_status: INCONSTRUCTIBLE -- any feasible unification changes at least one on-disk artefact (LRU JSON presence, sidecar write ordering, dir-fsync presence, lock primitive choice, init-time error contract) for at least one caller. The IMPLEMENTATION_RULES guard 'если behavioural-equivalence невозможна -- DEFER' fires.
microbench_status: NOT_RUN -- precondition failed (no behavioural-equivalent refactor to bench)
files_modified: audit/critique_2026_05_24/architectural_proposals/A5-discovery-suite-joint-stash.md (re-DEFER verdict + 6-point divergence detail + W15c follow-up section)
future_unification_prerequisites: Pick ONE canonical write path (recommend: safe_dump everywhere; accept SuiteArtefactCache atomicity model as canonical because it covers the .pkl + sidecar pair in one operation)

Pick ONE canonical LRU model (recommend: disk JSON via PIDAwareFileLock; SuiteArtefactCache accepts the per-put filelock cost since its workloads are minutes-per-call PySR symbolic FE -- the JSON write overhead is noise)
Pick ONE canonical lock primitive (recommend: PIDAwareFileLock; DiscoveryCache loses the no-filelock fallback but the optional-dep ergonomics are a relic from before pyutilz dependence was mandatory)
Pick ONE canonical key-sanitisation policy (recommend: require hex from callers; remove DiscoveryCache._safe_key + provide migration utility for operator caches with non-hex stems)
Ship migration logic for existing on-disk caches in operator HOME directories (mtimes-only -> JSON; mlframe-internal-format)
Multi-wave effort: spec wave + impl wave + migration wave + observability wave
